# Transcrição: Use_your_environment_to_build_better_habits

## [00:00 - 01:23]

I can get into a flow state to work very easily now. Generally, I can hit three hour blocks every day. My first three hours is very effective and there are habits in that. You are part of the process to help me form those. But I start always with the breathing technique that you've taught me. The Wim Hof technique. Two cycles of it was sufficient for me as a form of meditation and a way to really wake myself up. But then you gave me a gift, a diffuser. And that diffuser now has become a part of a habit. I have now anchored not only those actions of breathing and meditating, but I've also anchored a smell as well to me getting into that flow state. And on top of that, I also have anchored certain soundtracks. So to me, I put on a certain type of music. I put on a diffuser for a certain smell and I get myself into the state and into the workflow with that breathing technique. And then the next three hours for me is extremely productive. One of the things that stood out when you were talking about that is environment. You're setting the optimal conditions. It's making it easy, making it attractive, right? It goes back to the core principles of how do you stick to habits and how do you create a habit that will be sticky? An environment is a huge one. I mean, for me, even temperature is a huge one that I forgot to mention. I love it when it's slightly cold. To me, I work better. It's such a powerful reminder to us that make your environment work for you.
